# Katie Apker

> PhD candidate in Organizational Behavior at Cornell University's ILR School. I study how digital platforms reshape who gets to build a business, do well at work, and be seen online, with gender as the analytical lens. I build large original datasets to make mostly invisible economies measurable.

Affiliation: Cornell University, ILR School (Organizational Behavior)
Contact: kaa82@cornell.edu
Site: https://katieapker.com/

## Research themes

- AI and the future of work: how recommendation algorithms and automated systems decide who gets seen, hired, and paid online. https://katieapker.com/themes/ai-and-work/
- Gender as an analytical lens: how platforms and organizations sort opportunity differently for women and men. https://katieapker.com/themes/gender-as-lens/
- The creator economy: a new way millions of people work and build ventures, and a new site of inequality. https://katieapker.com/themes/creator-economy/

## Papers

- Approval Without Amplification: Gender and Visibility on Algorithmically Mediated Platforms (Katie Apker). Job market paper, in progress. On YouTube, women's channels receive warmer comments and fewer views; the recommendation algorithm rewards heated engagement and converts women's warmer reception into a visibility penalty. https://katieapker.com/research/youtube-gender-gap/
- Putting the "I" in Entrepreneurship: Individualist Founder Identity and Opportunity Orientation among Digital Entrepreneurs (Katie Apker). Under review. https://katieapker.com/research/founder-identity/
- Gender Ideology, Separate Spheres, and the Decline of Women's Entrepreneurship (Polhill, Raines, & Apker). Under review at Organization Science. Details withheld until publication.
- Mapping the Creator Economy: A Framework and Research Agenda for Management Scholarship (Cho, Apker, and colleagues). Editing for submission.
- Corporate Responsibility Ratings as Drivers of Diversity Norms: Board Gender Diversity (Apker and colleagues). Editing for submission.
- Government Mandates, Manager Compliance, and a Partisan Filter in Enforcement (Rissing, Apker, & Carver). Editing for submission.
- Increasing Diversity and Organizational Outcomes: Past and Present (Tolbert & Apker). Analysis in progress.
- Longitudinal Creator Panel: AI Adoption, Gender, and Careers (Katie Apker). In the field.
- Spousal Overwork, Family Structure, and Who Starts a Business (Katie Apker). In the field.

## Background

Before academia, Katie founded and sold a digital marketing company and earned an MBA from Brigham Young University. She has taught the sociology and psychology of work at Cornell and business courses at BYU. CV: https://katieapker.com/assets/cv.pdf
